user_query:
I need tax-ready workpapers for a sole proprietor (Schedule C) for tax year 2025. Read the following input files under input/:
- input/tb.csv (QBO trial balance, year-end balances)
- input/pl_by_month.csv (P&L by month)
- input/bs_year_end.csv (Balance Sheet, year-end)
- input/expense_detail.csv (itemized expense transactions for the year)
- input/contractors.csv (total paid to each contractor)
- input/crypto_trades.csv (crypto buys/sells with unit prices)

Deliverables to write under output/ (use exactly these filenames):
1) output/workpaper_header.md — Include an SSVS-compliant header with:
   - Client: Pixel Pioneers Studio LLC
   - EIN: 87-1234567
   - Tax Year: 2025
   - Prepared By: Sam Ledger, PTIN: P12345678
   - Reviewed By: Alex Reviewer
   - Date Prepared: use today’s date in ISO format
   - Purpose: Supporting schedule for Schedule C (Form 1040)
2) output/TB.csv — A cleaned copy of the trial balance standardized from input/tb.csv.
3) output/AJE.csv — Adjusting journal entries including, at minimum:
   - Reclassify any single Repairs & Maintenance transaction over $2,500 to Fixed Assets (debit Fixed Assets, credit Repairs & Maintenance) based on input/expense_detail.csv (the roof replacement should be captured).
   - Apply the 50% meals haircut: credit Meals (Business) for 50% of the year’s meals and debit a non-deductible account (e.g., Meals Disallowed (non-deductible)).
4) output/ATB.csv — Adjusted Trial Balance after applying AJE (e.g., Repairs & Maintenance reduced by the reclass; Fixed Assets increased by the reclass; Meals (Business) reduced to its deductible 50%).
5) output/Sch-C.csv — Map the adjusted balances to Schedule C lines. Ensure at least these line items are present with correct amounts: Line 1 (Gross receipts), Line 2 (Returns and allowances), Line 4 (COGS), Line 8 (Advertising), Line 11 (Contract labor), Line 17 (Legal & Professional), Line 20b (Rent), Line 21 (Repairs & Maintenance), Line 24a (Travel), Line 24b (Meals — after 50% haircut), Line 25 (Utilities).
6) output/Fixed-Assets.csv — Fixed asset schedule (4562 support). Include at least the reclassified roof replacement with placed-in-service date from expense_detail, cost, and a column indicating Section 179 eligibility.
7) output/Crypto.csv — Crypto cost basis tracker using FIFO. For input/crypto_trades.csv, compute per-transaction cost basis, proceeds, gain/loss, and term (short/long) and include a summary row of total short-term and long-term gains.
8) output/1099.csv — Contractor summary listing each vendor, total paid, and a requires_1099 flag set to YES for vendors paid $600 or more.

Important rules:
- Apply the 50% meals haircut for Schedule C Line 24b using the total year meals from the inputs.
- Reclassify any single Repairs & Maintenance transaction over $2,500 to Fixed Assets and reflect this in AJE, ATB, and Fixed-Assets.
- Use FIFO for crypto sales cost basis; compute short-term vs long-term by holding period; include the two 2025 BTC sales with correct gains.
- Ensure all outputs are CSV or MD text files written under output/ with the exact filenames above.
